perturbation.original_text: Neither the Franchisor, its Predecessor nor any person listed under Item 2 or an affiliate offering franchises under Franchisor's principal trademark: (A) has an administrative, criminal or civil action pending against that person alleging: a felony; a violation of a franchise, antitrust or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion; misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable civil or misdemeanor allegations.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither the Franchisor, its Licensee nor any person listed under Item 2 or an affiliate offering franchises under Franchisor's principal trademark: (A) has an administrative, criminal or civil action pending against that person alleging: a felony; a violation of a franchise, antitrust or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion; misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable civil or misdemeanor allegations.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Predecessor' to 'Licensee' introduces ambiguity. 'Predecessor' has a specific legal meaning referring to a previous business entity. 'Licensee' refers to an entity that holds a license to operate. The original text implies a check on past legal issues of the franchisor's predecessors while the modified text could be interpreted as referring to current Licensees. The term 'Franchisor's principal trademark' remains unchanged, creating further potential conflict because the document focuses on franchise agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LITIGATION Neither the Franchisor, its Licensee nor any person listed under Item 2 or an affiliate offering franchises under Franchisor's principal trademark: (A) has an administrative, criminal or civil action pending against that person alleging: a felony; a violation of a franchise, antitrust or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion; misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable civil or misdemeanor allegations. (B) has been convicted of a felony or pleaded nolo contendere to a felony charge or, within the ten year period immediately preceding the application for registration, has been convicted of or pleaded nolo contendere to a misdemeanor charge or has been the subject of a civil action alleging: violation of a franchise; anti-fraud or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion or misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable allegations. (C) is subject to a currently effective injunctive or restrictive order or decree relating to the franchise, or under a Federal, State or Canadian franchise, securities, antitrust, trade regulation or trade practice law, resulting from a concluded or pending action or proceeding brought by a public agency; or is subject to any currently effective order of any national securities association or national securities exchange, as defined in the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, suspending or expelling such person from membership in such association or exchange; or is subject to a currently effective injunctive or restrictive order relating to any other business activity as a result of an action brought by a public agency or department, including, without limitation, actions affecting a license as a real estate broker or sales agent.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Predecessor' to 'Licensee' introduces a contradiction because the original text aims to check for past legal issues of the franchisor's predecessors, while the modified text focuses on current Licensees. The contradicted text uses the same phrasing as the changed text ('Neither the Franchisor, its Licensee...'), but in the context of defining litigation history, the alteration from 'Predecessor' to 'Licensee' fundamentally changes the scope of the inquiry. This creates an inconsistency in who is being checked for prior litigation, leading to a strong in-text contradiction and making it unclear which entities' legal histories are relevant to the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Neither the Franchisor, its affiliate, its predecessor, officers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code; or (c) was a principal officer of a company or a general partner in a partnership that either filed as a debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code or that obtained a discharge of its debts under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code during or within one year after the officer or general partner of the Franchisor held this position in the company or partnership.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither the Franchisor, its associate, its predecessor, managers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code; or (c) was a principal officer of a company or a general partner in a partnership that either filed as a debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code or that obtained a discharge of its debts under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code during or within one year after the officer or general partner of the Franchisor held this position in the company or partnership.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'affiliate' with 'associate' and 'officers' with 'managers' introduces uncertainty. 'Affiliate' has a specific legal definition concerning corporate control. 'Associate' is more general. Similarly, 'officers' refers to specific corporate roles with defined responsibilities. 'Managers' has a broader meaning and might not encompass all 'officers'. This creates uncertainty about who is subject to the bankruptcy checks. The mention of 'general partner' remains, creating a contradiction between clearly defined roles and vaguely defined 'managers'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BANKRUPTCY Neither the Franchisor, its associate, its predecessor, managers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code; or (c) was a principal officer of a company or a general partner in a partnership that either filed as a debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code or that obtained a discharge of its debts under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code during or within one year after the officer or general partner of the Franchisor held this position in the company or partnership.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'affiliate' with 'associate' and 'officers' with 'managers.' The term 'affiliate' has a precise legal meaning, while 'associate' is broader. 'Officers' has a defined scope, unlike 'managers'. This introduces ambiguity about who the bankruptcy check applies to. Since the 'Contradicted Text' is an exact copy of the changed text, this means the ambiguity is now present in the 'Contradicted Text.' The presence of specific roles such as 'general partner' alongside the more ambiguous 'managers' creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether all individuals in these roles are subject to the same bankruptcy checks or obligations. This misalignment of terminology, as described in the definition, creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: MASTER FRANCHISEE: THE GRILLED CHEESE TRUCK, INC. By: Name: Title:
perturbation.changed_text: AREA DEVELOPER: THE GRILLED CHEESE TRUCK, INC. By: Name: Title:
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'MASTER FRANCHISEE' to 'AREA DEVELOPER' creates a conflict in the document. These are two distinct roles in franchising. A Master Franchisee typically has the right to sub-franchise within a territory, while an Area Developer is usually responsible for developing a certain number of units in a specific area. The text does not define either role, leading to confusion about the responsibilities and rights of 'THE GRILLED CHEESE TRUCK, INC'. This also leads to in-text contradiction since the name is implying that they have the rights of a MASTER FRANCHISEE by using the term FRANCHISE AGREEMENT, and then it calls for a confusing of the terminology when its stated as AREA DEVELOPER
perturbation.contradicted_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have duly executed, sealed and delivered this Addendum dated this ______ day of ______________, 2015. 2 Source: SOUPMAN, INC., 8-K, 8/14/2015 ATTEST KIOSK CONCEPTS, INC.: By: Witness Name: Title: AREA DEVELOPER: THE GRILLED CHEESE TRUCK, INC. By: Name: Title: Witness 3 Source: SOUPMAN, INC., 8-K, 8/14/2015
perturbation.justification: The change from 'MASTER FRANCHISEE' to 'AREA DEVELOPER' in the signing block creates a contradiction because the contract's contradicted text also includes the term 'AREA DEVELOPER' in another signing block, while the document uses the term 'FRANCHISE AGREEMENT'. This implies 'THE GRILLED CHEESE TRUCK, INC.' has the rights and responsibilities of both a master franchisee (due to 'FRANCHISE AGREEMENT') and an area developer. As stated in the explanation, these are two distinct roles. This creates ambiguity and inconsistency as the document does not define either role, leading to a conflict regarding the rights and responsibilities of the party. Therefore, it falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
